title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a message from an optician’s receptionist.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Hello Mr Patel, this is Bright View Opticians. You have an eye test booked for Friday afternoon. The optician you usually see is away, but another member of staff can see you. ||Woman: [1]There has been a cancellation at eleven thirty that morning, and we wondered whether you could come then instead of at four[/1]. Please call us today to confirm. Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Hello Mr Patel, this is Bright View Opticians. You have an eye test booked for Friday afternoon. The optician you usually see is away, but another member of staff can see you.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: There has been a cancellation at eleven thirty that morning, and we wondered whether you could come then instead of at four. Please call us today to confirm. Thank you.

questions.question: Why is the receptionist contacting Mr Patel?

options.A: to offer him an earlier appointment
options.B: to cancel his eye test completely
options.C: to ask him to see his usual optician

questions.explanation: She offers a cancelled appointment at eleven thirty instead of his afternoon booking (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a weekend market.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you buy anything at the craft market? I know you wanted some local cheese. ||Man: I tried plenty, but I didn’t take any home. There were loads of stalls selling food, though. ||Woman: So what’s in the bag? ||Man: [2]A necklace made from coloured glass. My sister’s birthday is next week, and it looked much more original than anything in the shops[/2].

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you buy anything at the craft market? I know you wanted some local cheese.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I tried plenty, but I didn’t take any home. There were loads of stalls selling food, though.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So what’s in the bag?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: A necklace made from coloured glass. My sister’s birthday is next week, and it looked much more original than anything in the shops.

questions.question: What did the man buy at the market?
questions.explanation: He bought a glass necklace as a birthday present for his sister (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear an announcement at a railway station.
extracts.audio_script: Guide: Passengers for the 14.05 train to York, please note that the train is delayed by approximately ten minutes because of signalling problems. It will still leave from platform seven. ||Guide: [3]Passengers travelling beyond York to Malton should speak to staff at the information desk, as their connecting train will leave before this service arrives[/3]. We apologise for the inconvenience.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: Passengers for the 14.05 train to York, please note that the train is delayed by approximately ten minutes because of signalling problems. It will still leave from platform seven.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: Passengers travelling beyond York to Malton should speak to staff at the information desk, as their connecting train will leave before this service arrives. We apologise for the inconvenience.

questions.question: Which passengers should speak to station staff?

options.C: those waiting on the wrong platform

questions.explanation: Passengers going beyond York may miss their connection and are told to contact staff (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio interview with a young gardener.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: Daniel, you work as a gardener in a large hotel. What’s the best part of your job? ||Man: I’m outdoors most of the day, which is nice, and I’ve learnt a lot about plants. But [4]what I really love is returning to an area after I’ve worked on it and immediately seeing how much better it looks[/4]. Guests sometimes say nice things, too, but that’s a bonus.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Daniel, you work as a gardener in a large hotel. What’s the best part of your job?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’m outdoors most of the day, which is nice, and I’ve learnt a lot about plants. But what I really love is returning to an area after I’ve worked on it and immediately seeing how much better it looks. Guests sometimes say nice things, too, but that’s a bonus.

questions.question: What does Daniel like most about his work?

options.C: seeing the results of his efforts

questions.explanation: He most enjoys seeing the improvement after he has worked on an area (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer hiring a bicycle.
extracts.audio_script: Assistant: Would you like the city bike or the mountain bike for your afternoon ride? ||Customer: The city bike will be fine. I’m only following the riverside path, so I won’t need anything special. ||Assistant: A helmet is included, and locks are two pounds extra. ||Customer: [5]I’ll take a lock, please. I’m planning to stop for lunch, and I don’t want to keep the bike beside my table[/5].

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: Would you like the city bike or the mountain bike for your afternoon ride?

dialogue.speaker: customer
dialogue.text: The city bike will be fine. I’m only following the riverside path, so I won’t need anything special.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: A helmet is included, and locks are two pounds extra.

dialogue.speaker: customer
dialogue.text: I’ll take a lock, please. I’m planning to stop for lunch, and I don’t want to keep the bike beside my table.

questions.question: What extra item does the customer decide to hire?
questions.explanation: The customer asks for a lock because he plans to leave the bicycle while having lunch (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear two students discussing a revision group.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you joining the science revision group after class today? I’m not sure it’s worth it if everyone just reads their notes quietly. ||Woman: That’s not what happened last week. We worked in pairs and asked each other questions. ||Man: Did that help? ||Woman: Yes. [6]Explaining an answer to somebody else showed me exactly which parts I hadn’t understood properly myself[/6]. You should come this time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you joining the science revision group after class today? I’m not sure it’s worth it if everyone just reads their notes quietly.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That’s not what happened last week. We worked in pairs and asked each other questions.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did that help?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. Explaining an answer to somebody else showed me exactly which parts I hadn’t understood properly myself. You should come this time.

questions.question: Why does the woman recommend the revision group?

options.A: It helps her identify gaps in her knowledge.
options.C: It gives students a quiet place to work.

questions.explanation: She says explaining answers helped her recognise what she did not fully understand (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a meeting.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you booked the large meeting room for the client visit tomorrow? ||Woman: I tried, but another team has it all morning. The smaller room isn’t big enough for everyone. ||Man: Could we move the meeting to Thursday? ||Woman: The client’s only in town tomorrow. [7]I’ve arranged for our colleagues in Manchester to join online instead, so the smaller room will be fine[/7]. ||Man: Good solution.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you booked the large meeting room for the client visit tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I tried, but another team has it all morning. The smaller room isn’t big enough for everyone.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Could we move the meeting to Thursday?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The client’s only in town tomorrow. I’ve arranged for our colleagues in Manchester to join online instead, so the smaller room will be fine.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good solution.

questions.question: How has the woman solved the problem?

options.A: She has changed the day of the meeting.
options.B: She has reduced the number attending in person.
options.C: She has found a larger meeting room.

questions.explanation: Colleagues from Manchester will join online, reducing the number in the small room (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio presenter talking about a community cinema.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: The Park Community Cinema shows classic films every Thursday evening and is looking for people to help welcome visitors and sell programmes. Volunteers will work from six until nine, with training given before their first evening. ||Woman: [8]In return for helping at two screenings each month, volunteers can choose one film ticket free of charge[/8]. Application forms are available at the cinema entrance.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The Park Community Cinema shows classic films every Thursday evening and is looking for people to help welcome visitors and sell programmes. Volunteers will work from six until nine, with training given before their first evening.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: In return for helping at two screenings each month, volunteers can choose one film ticket free of charge. Application forms are available at the cinema entrance.

questions.question: What benefit is offered to cinema volunteers?

options.C: a free ticket after regular help

questions.explanation: Volunteers who help at two screenings a month can receive a free film ticket (evidence [8]).
